content.text:
Both output files are generated and verified. 

**Results:**
- **`/app/merged_users.parquet`** — 4 unique users (101–104), one row each. `user_id` is `int64`; `name`/`email`/`created_date`/`status` are strings; all dates match `YYYY-MM-DD`.
- **`/app/conflicts.json`** — `total_conflicts: 3`, all for user 101 (present in all three sources) on `name`, `email`, and `created_date`, each resolved to the source_a value.

**How it works:** each source's raw keys are mapped to unified names via `FIELD_MAP` (e.g. `id`/`user_id`/`userId` → `user_id`), records are indexed by `user_id`, and per field the highest-priority non-null value (A > B > C) is selected. A conflict is logged whenever ≥2 sources supply differing values for a field. Users 102/103/104 appear in a single source (no conflicts); `status` exists only in source_a, so users 103/104 have `None`.

One design note worth flagging: `status` is only defined as a string field in source_a — sources B/C carry boolean `is_active`/`active` fields, which the spec's field-mapping table does not list, so I did not map them into `status`. If you want those booleans folded into `status`, that's a one-line mapping change.

content.text: No test suite exists for this data task, so I'll run a validation check against every success criterion as the verification.

input.command:
python3 - <<'PYEOF'
import json, re, csv
import pandas as pd

df = pd.read_parquet("/app/merged_users.parquet")
rep = json.load(open("/app/conflicts.json"))

# Gather expected unique user_ids from all sources
ids = set()
ids |= {r["id"] for r in json.load(open("/data/source_a/users.json"))}
with open("/data/source_b/users.csv", newline="") as f:
    ids |= {int(r["user_id"]) for r in csv.DictReader(f)}
ids |= set(pd.read_parquet("/data/source_c/users.parquet")["userId"].astype(int))

checks = []
checks.append(("all unique users present", set(df["user_id"]) == ids and len(df)==len(ids)))
checks.append(("user_id is integer", str(df["user_id"].dtype) in ("int64","int32")))
checks.append(("columns correct", list(df.columns)==["user_id","name","email","created_date","status"]))
checks.append(("dates YYYY-MM-DD", df["created_date"].dropna().map(lambda s: bool(re.match(r"^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$", s))).all()))
checks.append(("no duplicate users", df["user_id"].is_unique))
# priority resolution: user 101 fields must equal source_a
a = {r["id"]:r for r in json.load(open("/data/source_a/users.json"))}[101]
row101 = df[df["user_id"]==101].iloc[0]
checks.append(("priority A wins for 101 name", row101["name"]==a["full_name"]))
checks.append(("priority A wins for 101 email", row101["email"]==a["email"]))
checks.append(("priority A wins for 101 date", row101["created_date"]==a["registration_date"]))
checks.append(("total_conflicts matches list", rep["total_conflicts"]==len(rep["conflicts"])))
checks.append(("each conflict selected == highest-priority value",
    all(c["selected"]==next(c["values"][s] for s in ["source_a","source_b","source_c"] if s in c["values"]) for c in rep["conflicts"])))
checks.append(("each conflict has differing values", all(len(set(c["values"].values()))>1 for c in rep["conflicts"])))

ok = all(v for _,v in checks)
for name, v in checks:
    print(("PASS" if v else "FAIL"), name)
print("\nALL PASSED" if ok else "\nFAILURES PRESENT")
PYEOF
